bullets.text: Brent crude hits $100 as Iran’s new leader vows Strait of Hormuz stays closed, intensifying global supply risk and inflation pressures.
U.S. Navy plans tanker escorts through Hormuz when feasible, signaling heightened militarization of energy trade routes and persistent disruption risk.
U.S. temporarily allows purchases of Russian oil already at sea to stabilize markets, effectively easing enforcement amid a Middle East supply shock.
Oil shock from Iran conflict adds another hit to global growth, worsening inflation-versus-recession tradeoffs for central banks and governments.
Markets are rapidly repricing: expectations for near-term Fed rate cuts fade, raising the bar for risk assets as inflation risks re-emerge.
Private credit stress deepens: defaults hit a record 9.2% in 2025, amplifying worries about leveraged borrowers and spillovers to banks and markets.
U.S. launches fresh Section 301 probes into 60 economies over forced-labor practices, signaling broader trade friction and potential new tariffs.
Fed approves Home BancShares application, highlighting ongoing supervisory gatekeeping for bank expansion amid tighter funding and credit conditions.
Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen to step down after successor installed, adding leadership-transition risk during intense AI-driven product and pricing shifts.
Meta delays rollout of a new AI model over performance concerns, underscoring reliability bottlenecks and rising competitive pressure in frontier model releases.
Ukraine will provide drone-combat videos for AI training, accelerating military AI development while raising data-governance and escalation concerns.
NASA says Artemis II remains on track for April 1, a key milestone for crewed lunar operations and for validating deep-space mission systems.
Researchers identify blood-protein patterns that may detect Alzheimer’s earlier, potentially enabling scalable screening and earlier intervention trials.
A new “super antibiotic” targets deadly gut infection without wiping out the microbiome, a potential breakthrough against resistance and collateral damage.
NASA’s DART mission altered an asteroid’s orbit around the Sun, strengthening planetary-defense feasibility beyond binary impact tests.
